framework: Schelling focal point detection + physical observables
design: 两层观测: 物理层(状态)+ 焦点层(转折). History in geopulse.db

history.est: 1-2 declining (IRGC baseline) BUT 100+ missile joint salvo Apr 1 with Houthis+Hezbollah
history.note: Day 42. ★★★★★ PATTERN BREAK — JOINT SALVO: (1) IRGC launched NEW coordinated wave: 100+ heavy missiles + attack drones + 200+ rockets — JOINT with Houthis + Hezbollah (Moneycontrol/TheHindu Apr 1); (2) Houthis '3rd military operation targeting sensitive Israeli targets with ballistic missiles' — jointly with Iran + Hezbollah (Yahya Saree statement); (3) This is SIGNIFICANT REVERSAL from 1-2/day declining pattern — suggests coordinated multi-front salvo capability REMAINS; (4) Trump claims Pezeshkian asked for ceasefire (TruthSocial Apr 1) — Iran calls it 'false, baseless'; (5) IRGC 18-company deadline at 20:00 Tehran Apr 1 — UNTESTED at pipeline time; (6) A-10 Warthogs deploying to Hormuz as ground operation option emerges (ArmyRecognition Apr 1); (7) Argentina designated IRGC terrorist org (Mar 31); (8) US KIA: 15+ confirmed + 300+ wounded; (9) IDF continuing Tehran strikes; (10) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed. CRITICAL: Joint salvo BREAKS the 1-2/day pattern — must monitor if this is one-off coordinated show-of-force or return to mass salvos. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts CONFIRMED ACTIVE simultaneously. Source: Moneycontrol/TheHindu/ArmyRecognition/Reuters/CNBC/Axios

history.est: 1-2 declining (IRGC baseline) BUT Apr 1 joint salvo 100+ missiles confirmed, 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ TRUMP ADDRESS + PARADOX: (1) Trump primetime address Apr 1: 'core strategic objectives nearing completion', 'leaving very soon', 2-3 week timeframe to deal or bombard infrastructure (CNBC/NYT/AP/Politico/LAT); (2) BUT Iran FM denies ALL negotiations — 'false and baseless' re ceasefire claim (AJE/USAToday); (3) IRGC Apr 1 joint salvo 100+ heavy missiles + drones WITH Houthis+Hezbollah CONFIRMED (Moneycontrol/TheHindu); (4) IRGC 18-company deadline Apr 1 20:00 Tehran PASSED — no confirmed corporate attacks YET, monitoring; (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — launched ballistic missiles at 'sensitive' Israeli targets, intercepted (Wikipedia Apr 2); (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (7) US KIA: 13 (NYT/Pentagon) to 15 (Wikipedia) + 300+ wounded (PBS); (8) Intercept Apr 1: Pentagon covering up Iran war casualties — 15 US troops wounded Friday Saudi base attack alone; (9) CENTCOM 10,000+ targets, 2/3 arms mfg destroyed; (10) Hegseth CONCEDED Iran retains strike capability. PATTERN: Trump declaring near-victory WHILE 3 independent fronts active + joint 100+ salvo CONTRADICTS wind-down. Apr 6 energy deadline 4 days. Source: CNBC/NYT/AP/Politico/LAT/AJE/USAToday/Moneycontrol/PBS/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2 declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo CONFIRMED, 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained, Trump: 'extremely hard' 2-3 weeks
history.note: Day 33/Day 43. ★★★★★ POST-ADDRESS ESCALATION: (1) IRGC fire STILL 1-2/day baseline — mass salvo era over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo with Houthis+Hezbollah = PATTERN BREAK; (2) NYT Apr 2: Trump claims success, NO CLEAR TIMELINE, vows to hit Iran 'extremely hard over next 2-3 weeks'; (3) ISW Mar 31: Iran shipbuilding complex (ISOICO) + IRGC Navy R&D struck near Bandar Abbas; (4) UAE intercepted 36 drones + 8 BM + 4 cruise missiles on Mar 31 alone (ISW); (5) Iran drone hit telecom building in Sharjah; (6) Houthis SUSTAINED 2+ waves independently — 3rd joint military operation with Iran+Hezbollah (Yahya Saree); (7) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (8) 1,260+ Lebanese killed (NYT); (9) IRGC 18-company deadline PASSED Apr 1 20:00 Tehran — NO confirmed corporate attacks as of Apr 2; (10) Intercept Apr 1: Pentagon COVERING UP casualty figures; US KIA 13-15 confirmed + 300+ wounded; (11) CENTCOM 10,000+ targets, ISOICO destroyed. PATTERN: IRGC baseline collapsed BUT Apr 1 joint salvo = coordinated multi-front capability REMAINS. Trump rhetoric intensifying ('extremely hard') while claiming near-victory. Source: NYT/ISW/Intercept/Reuters/PBS/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo CONFIRMED (Houthis+Hezbollah), 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained, IDF 15 weapons-production sites struck Apr 1
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED + ISW/IDF CONFIRMED ACTIONS: (1) IRGC fire STILL 1-2/day baseline — mass salvo era over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo with Houthis+Hezbollah (Moneycontrol/TheHindu); (2) IDF struck ~15 weapons production sites Apr 1 incl MODAFL advanced missile complex (ISW Apr 1); (3) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM launched toward Beersheba (Wikipedia Apr 2), vowed to continue 'until aggression on all resistance fronts stops'; (4) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (5) Iran says has 'necessary will' to end war but seeks guarantees (ToI Apr 2); (6) NatSec Dems (Smith/Meeks/Himes) demand Trump announce ceasefire negotiation (House Armed Services Apr 1); (7) Intercept: Pentagon COVERING UP casualty figures — 15 US troops wounded Friday Saudi base alone; (8) US KIA: 13-15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Intercept Apr 1 citing 520+); (9) IRGC 18-company deadline PASSED Apr 1 20:00 Tehran — NO confirmed corporate attacks; (10) IEA warns oil supply crunch will WORSEN in April (CNBC Apr 1). PATTERN: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign continuing at high tempo but FAILING to eliminate Iran strike capability. Source: ISW/IDF/ToI/Intercept/CNBC/Wikipedia/Moneycontrol/House Armed Services

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline) BUT Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah CONFIRMED; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300 sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized'
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ CENTCOM WEEK 5 + JOINT SALVO AFTERMATH: (1) CENTCOM Commander Cooper Apr 2: 12,300 sites struck in 5 weeks, 'substantial portion of Iran military capabilities completely neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates); (2) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — mass salvo era CONFIRMED over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ heavy missiles+drones WITH Houthis+Hezbollah (Moneycontrol/TheHindu); (3) IDF struck ~15 weapons production sites Apr 1 incl MODAFL advanced missile complex (ISW); (4) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba Apr 2, vowed to continue 'until aggression stops' (Wikipedia); (5) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (6) Trump primetime Apr 1: 'core strategic objectives nearing completion' BUT threatens 'extremely hard' 2-3 weeks (NYT/CNBC); (7) Iran FM: 'false and baseless' re ceasefire claim (AJE); (8) IRGC 18-company deadline PASSED Apr 1 20:00 — NO confirmed corporate attacks; (9) US KIA: 13-15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Intercept); (10) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed. PATTERN: IRGC volume collapsed BUT Apr 1 joint salvo = coordinated multi-front capability REMAINS. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). CENTCOM claims near-victory but Iran retains strike ability (Hegseth concession). Source: VoiceOfEmirates/CENTCOM/NYT/CNBC/AJE/ISW/Intercept/Moneycontrol/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo aftermath, CENTCOM Wk5 12,300 sites, WTI +11% Apr 2 Trump speech
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ CENTCOM WK5 + MARKET SHOCK: (1) CENTCOM Cooper Apr 2: 12,300 sites struck in 5 weeks, 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates/AJE); (2) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah = ONE-OFF coordinated show of force, NOT return to mass salvos; (3) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba Apr 2 (Wikipedia); (4) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (5) WTI SPIKED 11% to USD111.54 on Trump Apr 2 speech vowing 'extremely hard' 2-3 weeks (CNBC Apr 2); (6) Brent USD109.03 (+8% Apr 2); (7) Trump Apr 1 primetime: 'nearing completion' BUT threatens escalation — CONTRADICTION; (8) IRGC 18-company deadline PASSED Apr 1 20:00 — NO confirmed corporate attacks; (9) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Intercept); (10) Hegseth STILL concedes Iran retains strike ability. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign week 5 failing to eliminate capability. Apr 6 energy deadline 4 days. Source: CNBC/VoiceOfEmirates/AJE/NYT/Wikipedia/Intercept

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo CONFIRMED one-off, US struck Iran's largest bridge Apr 2 (8 killed 95 wounded)
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ BRIDGE STRIKE + SUSTAINED MULTI-FRONT: (1) US/Israel STRUCK Iran's largest bridge near Karaj Apr 2 — 8 killed, 95 wounded, Trump: 'more to follow' (Guardian/BBC Apr 2); (2) CENTCOM Apr 2 video: strikes on drones/tanks/missile launchers, 12,300+ sites struck week 5 (AJE/CENTCOM); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — mass salvo era over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah = ONE-OFF coordinated show-of-force; (4) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba (Wikipedia Apr 2); (5) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (6) CENTCOM: 155 Iranian vessels damaged/destroyed; (7) US KIA: 13 (Pentagon/NYT) to 15 (Wikipedia) + 520+ wounded (Intercept: Pentagon COVERING UP figures); (8) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed; (9) WTI SPIKED 11% to USD111.54, Brent USD109.03 on Trump Apr 2 'extremely hard' speech; (10) Trump vows 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' WHILE claiming 'nearing completion'. PATTERN: Infrastructure targeting ESCALATING (bridge = civilian infrastructure). 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign week 5 FAILING to eliminate Iran strike capability (Hegseth concession). Source: Guardian/BBC/AJE/CENTCOM/CNBC/NYT/Intercept/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo confirmed one-off, US struck largest bridge Karaj Apr 2 (8 killed 95 wounded), CENTCOM Wk5 12,300 sites
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ WEEK 5 SUSTAINED MULTI-FRONT + INFRASTRUCTURE ESCALATION: (1) CENTCOM Apr 2: 12,300 sites struck in 5 weeks, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates/AJE); (2) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — mass salvo era CONFIRMED over EXCEPT Apr 1 joint 100+ heavy missiles+drones WITH Houthis+Hezbollah = ONE-OFF coordinated show-of-force (Moneycontrol/TheHindu); (3) US struck Iran LARGEST BRIDGE near Karaj Apr 2 — 8 killed, 95 wounded, Trump: 'more to follow' (Guardian/BBC Apr 2) = CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE targeting while claiming wind-down; (4) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM toward Beersheba Apr 2, vowed to continue (Wikipedia); (5) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (6) Brent SURGED past USD109 Apr 3 after Trump speech (BBC); (7) Trump Apr 2 vows 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' WHILE claiming near-victory = MAXIMUM PARADOX; (8) Hegseth STILL concedes Iran retains strike ability despite monthlong bombing (NYT); (9) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded, Pentagon COVERING UP (Intercept); (10) Iran: HRANA 3,486+ killed. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign week 5 FAILING to eliminate capability. Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS. Source: BBC/Guardian/VoiceOfEmirates/AJE/NYT/CNBC/Intercept/Wikipedia

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), tankers fleeing Dubai, NO US escort, Guardian: trickle via Iranian waters
history.note: Day 42. ★★★ TRANSIT UNCHANGED — POST-TANKER FALLOUT CONTINUES: (1) Guardian Mar 26: trickle of ships via alternative route through Iranian waters — 'Tehran's tollbooth'; (2) USNI Apr confirms: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 in 2025 = 94.6% decline; (3) NO US Navy escort completed — USNI April issue confirms; (4) Tankers FLEEING Dubai post-Al-Salmi attack (Guardian); (5) Iran FM Araghchi Apr 1: safe passage for 'friendly countries' vessels only; (6) Trump: ceasefire ONLY when Hormuz open (Reuters Apr 1) — makes strait THE precondition; (7) IRGC tolled passage via Larak Island (~USD2M/transit, yuan-denominated); (8) ~3,000 vessels still waiting; (9) COSCO 2 ships = only confirmed major transit; (10) Bessent: 'retake Hormuz' — rhetoric only, no action materialized. Still 95% below pre-crisis (17-20 mbd). Source: Guardian/USNI/Reuters/Axios/Bloomberg

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), 5-nation friendly transit (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK), yuan/crypto tolls, NO US escort, tankers fleeing Dubai
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ SELECTIVE REOPENING + TOLL REGIME: (1) Iran FM Araghchi Mar 26: 5 'friendly nations' (China/Russia/India/Iraq/Pakistan) allowed to transit Hormuz (TheHindu/ToI/Wikipedia); (2) Bloomberg Apr 1: ships paying YUAN AND CRYPTO tolls for safe passage — 'Tehran's tollbooth' institutionalized; (3) USNI/Lloyd's: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025 = 94.6% decline UNCHANGED; (4) COSCO 2 state-backed ships = only confirmed major transit; (5) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi VLCC attack (Guardian); (6) NO US Navy escort completed — USNI April issue confirms; (7) BreakingDefense Apr 1: Hormuz disruption changing trade+defense at other chokepoints globally; (8) Brent FELL to ~ after Trump address from  peak — market pricing wind-down; (9) ~3,000 vessels waiting; (10) Iran parliament codifying Hormuz sovereignty+tolls (NPR). ★★★ CRITICAL: Two-tier Hormuz emerging — friendly nations transit with tolls vs Western shipping blocked. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: Bloomberg/TheHindu/Wikipedia/USNI/BreakingDefense/Guardian/NPR

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), COSCO 2 ULCVs confirmed, yuan/crypto tolls institutionalized, NO US escort, LPG Jag Vasant reached Mumbai
history.note: Day 43 D+33. ★★★★ TWO-TIER HORMUZ CONFIRMED: (1) Safety4Sea Apr 1: COSCO CSCL Indian Ocean + CSCL Arctic Ocean (ultra-large container vessels) SUCCESSFULLY transited Hormuz; (2) Anadolu Apr 1: LPG carrier Jag Vasant reached Mumbai Port after transiting Hormuz; (3) Bloomberg Apr 1: ships paying YUAN AND CRYPTO tolls — Tehran tollbooth INSTITUTIONALIZED; (4) 5 friendly nations (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK) transit regime confirmed (Araghchi Mar 26); (5) USNI April issue confirms: US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping; (6) USNI/Lloyd's: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025 = 94.6% decline; (7) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi VLCC attack (Guardian); (8) ~3,000 vessels waiting; (9) Iran parliament codifying Hormuz sovereignty+tolls (NPR); (10) TheConversation: securing Hormuz requires 1-2 naval ships per escort. ★★★ Two-tier system HARDENING: friendly-nation vessels transit with tolls, Western/hostile shipping BLOCKED. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: Safety4Sea/Anadolu/Bloomberg/USNI/Guardian/NPR

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), Iran INSTITUTIONALIZES Larak Island checkpoint, IRGC vets each ship + USD2M toll, NO US escort, yuan/crypto tolls formalized in law
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ IRAN FORMALIZES HORMUZ CONTROL: (1) CNBC Apr 2: Iran turns Larak Island into oil CHECKPOINT — select vessels routed through Iranian territorial waters near Larak, IRGC+port authorities vet each ship before granting passage; (2) Ships must provide IMO number, cargo manifest, crew names, ownership, destination to IRGC intermediaries — receive code + 20-mile corridor instructions between Qeshm/Larak under IRGC escort; (3) HouseOfSaud: USD2M per ship transit fee; (4) Iran parliament FORMALIZED Hormuz tolls legislation (Newland Chase); (5) LPG Pine Gas routed through Larak channel, escorted by Indian warships (CNBC/Reuters); (6) AA: 'certain vessels granted permits' — limited maritime trade regime; (7) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping'; (8) NO US Navy escort completed through Hormuz (confirmed); (9) COSCO 2 ULCVs confirmed previously; (10) Mine countermeasures crisis ongoing (USNI). PATTERN: Iran INSTITUTIONALIZING de facto sovereignty over Hormuz — moving from emergency blockade to PERMANENT toll/checkpoint regime. US military unable/unwilling to break this. Source: CNBC/AA/USNI/HouseOfSaud/Newland Chase/Reuters

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), Iran FORMALIZES Larak checkpoint, IRGC vets+escorts each ship, USD2M toll, yuan/crypto, NO US escort, 5 friendly nations regime
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ IRAN INSTITUTIONALIZES HORMUZ CONTROL: (1) CNBC Apr 2: Iran turns Larak Island into oil CHECKPOINT — IRGC+port authorities vet each ship, require IMO/cargo/crew data, issue corridor code + 20-mile channel between Qeshm/Larak under IRGC escort; (2) USD2M per ship transit fee (HouseOfSaud); (3) Iran parliament FORMALIZED Hormuz tolls legislation (Newland Chase); (4) 5 friendly nations (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK) transit regime confirmed (Araghchi Mar 26); (5) COSCO 2 ULCVs + LPG Jag Vasant confirmed transits (Safety4Sea/Anadolu); (6) Bloomberg: yuan AND crypto tolls — Tehran tollbooth INSTITUTIONALIZED; (7) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping' — CONFIRMED; (8) Hegseth downplays risk but Lloyd's: recent transits still <10% of average (USNI Mar 31); (9) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi attack (Guardian); (10) ~3,000 vessels waiting. PATTERN: Two-tier Hormuz HARDENING — friendly nations transit with tolls+IRGC escort, Western/hostile shipping BLOCKED. Iran moving from emergency blockade to PERMANENT sovereignty regime. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: CNBC/USNI/Bloomberg/Safety4Sea/Anadolu/Guardian/HouseOfSaud/Newland Chase

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED, IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll, yuan/crypto, NO US escort, 5 friendly nations
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ TWO-TIER HORMUZ HARDENING: (1) CNBC Apr 2: Iran INSTITUTIONALIZED Larak Island checkpoint — IRGC+port authorities vet each ship (IMO/cargo/crew/ownership), issue corridor code + 20-mile channel; (2) Guardian Mar 26: 'Tehran's tollbooth' — ships paying USD2M per transit, yuan+crypto accepted; (3) Iran parliament FORMALIZED Hormuz tolls legislation (Newland Chase); (4) 5 friendly nations (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK) regime confirmed (Araghchi); (5) COSCO 2 ULCVs + LPG Jag Vasant = only confirmed major transits; (6) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping' — CONFIRMED; (7) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi VLCC attack (Guardian); (8) ~3,000 vessels waiting; (9) Trump Apr 1: ceasefire ONLY when Hormuz open — makes strait THE precondition; (10) TheConversation: securing Hormuz requires 1-2 naval ships per escort op. PATTERN: Iran moving from emergency blockade to PERMANENT toll/sovereignty regime. Two-tier: friendly nations transit with tolls+IRGC escort, Western/hostile BLOCKED. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: CNBC/Guardian/USNI/TheConversation/Newland Chase

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-6 ships/day), Iran drafting PERMANENT Hormuz protocol with Oman, Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED, 5 friendly nations + Malaysia + Philippines expanding, NO US escort
history.note: Day 44. ★★★★★ IRAN PERMANENT SOVEREIGNTY BID: (1) ★★★ NYT Apr 2: Iran Deputy FM Gharibabadi says Iran drafting PROTOCOL with Oman to 'oversee transit through Hormuz' EVEN IN PEACETIME — most significant sovereignty escalation yet; (2) NYT: Malaysia + Philippines announced Iran promised to allow their vessels = friendly-nation list EXPANDING beyond original 5 (CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (3) CNBC Apr 2: Iran INSTITUTIONALIZED Larak Island checkpoint — IRGC vets each ship (IMO/cargo/crew), issues corridor code + 20-mile channel under IRGC escort; (4) Bloomberg: yuan + crypto tolls — Tehran tollbooth formalized; (5) Iran parliament CODIFIED Hormuz tolls legislation; (6) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts of commercial shipping' — CONFIRMED; (7) Tankers STILL fleeing Dubai post-Al-Salmi VLCC attack; (8) ~3,000 vessels waiting; (9) USNI/Lloyd's: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025 = 94.6% decline UNCHANGED. ★★★ CRITICAL: Iran moving from WARTIME blockade to PERMANENT peacetime oversight regime. Two-tier Hormuz HARDENING: friendly+expanding nations transit with tolls+IRGC escort, Western/hostile BLOCKED. Still 95% below pre-crisis. Source: NYT/CNBC/Bloomberg/USNI/AA/Guardian

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day, Polymarket 98.3% for 0-10 ships Apr 3), Iran EXPANDING friendly-nation list (PH Apr 2), Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED, NO US escort
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRAN EXPANDING TOLL REGIME + TRANSIT STILL NEAR-ZERO: (1) HinduBusinessLine Apr 2: transit COLLAPSED from 150/day to 4-5/day — CONFIRMED; (2) Polymarket Apr 3: 98.3% probability of 0-10 ships today; (3) Iran EXPANDING friendly-nation list: Philippines Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK + Malaysia + Thailand) — widening two-tier system (Wikipedia/Philstar); (4) Most crossings follow Iran-approved routes (Philstar Apr 2); (5) Iran FORMALIZED Larak Island checkpoint — IRGC vets each ship (IMO/cargo/crew), USD2M toll, yuan+crypto (CNBC/HouseOfSaud); (6) Iran parliament CODIFIED Hormuz tolls legislation; (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT Hormuz oversight protocol being drafted (NYT Apr 2); (8) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — CONFIRMED 5 weeks in; (9) Tankers still fleeing Dubai post Al-Salmi VLCC attack; (10) 2,190 vessels with transponders in Gulf west of Hormuz (Bloomberg/Philstar) — massive backlog. PATTERN: Two-tier Hormuz HARDENING into PERMANENT regime. Friendly-nation list expanding but Western shipping BLOCKED. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis 120 transits/day. Source: HinduBusinessLine/Polymarket/Philstar/Wikipedia/CNBC/USNI/Bloomberg/NYT

q3_spr_release_rate.label: SPR实际释放速率(mbd)
q3_spr_release_rate.delivery_timeline: 120 days per DOE, first barrels end of W3

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed + NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound RFP Apr 1, D+26, SPR deliveries BEGIN today
history.note: Day 42 D+26. ★★★★ NEW SPR ACTION: (1) DOE issued NEW RFP for 10M bbl emergency exchange from Bryan Mound site (Reuters/DOE Apr 1) — ADDITIONAL to existing 45.2M first batch; (2) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global CERAWeek); (3) SPR crude deliveries OFFICIALLY BEGIN today Apr 1 to Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor (Argus); (4) 172M bbl US / 412M bbl global (IEA 32 countries); (5) Gas BREACHED USD4.018/gal national (CNBC/Axios/Reuters Mar 31), CA USD5.87/gal; (6) Brent USD104-115 range; WTI USD102-103; (7) S&P 500 surged 2.9% to 6,528 on de-escalation hopes Apr 1; (8) Argus: crude return Nov 2026-Sep 2028; (9) OPEC+ production unwind Apr 1. ★ Bryan Mound 10M RFP = signal DOE preparing to ACCELERATE draws beyond initial plan. SPR trickle still IRRELEVANT — 1.5 mbd vs 15-17 mbd Hormuz loss. Source: Reuters/DOE/Argus/CNBC/S&P Global

history.note: Day 43 D+28. ★★★★ SPR DELIVERIES ACTIVE + OIL PRICE REVERSAL: (1) SPR crude deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1 — Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor receiving (Argus/IndexBox); (2) DOE NEW 10M bbl emergency exchange RFP from Bryan Mound Apr 1, proposals due Apr 6 (Reuters/DOE); (3) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global CERAWeek); (4) 172M bbl US / 412M bbl global (IEA 32 countries); (5) ★★★ NYT Apr 2: oil prices JUMP + stocks TUMBLE after Trump vows 'extremely hard' 2-3 weeks — Brent ~USD107 (+6%); (6) Gas USD4.064/gal national Apr 2 (EconomicTimes/CNBC), CA USD5.87+; (7) Brent volatile: fell to ~USD101 on wind-down rhetoric then SPIKED to USD107 on escalation (NYT); (8) OPEC+ production unwind Apr 1; (9) Bryan Mound 10M RFP = DOE ACCELERATING beyond initial plan. history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE), G7 historic coordinated release, US 172M bbl, IEA warns April crunch worsening
history.note: Day 43. ★★★★★ G7 HISTORIC RELEASE BUT 'DROP IN BUCKET': (1) DOE/Chris Wright: 1-1.5 mbd release rate confirmed (IndexBox); (2) G7 announced historic coordinated emergency stockpile release (EnergyConnects); (3) US contributing 172M barrels — bulk of G7 supply (Chris Wright); (4) IEA Fatih Birol warns oil supply crunch will WORSEN in April (CNBC Apr 1); (5) FinancialContent: SPR volume considered 'drop in bucket' vs sustained Gulf production loss; (6) EnergiasMedia: structure enables SPR to be replenished with ~200M bbl over time (20% more than released); (7) New 10M bbl Bryan Mound RFP due Apr 6; (8) SPR max capacity 4.4 mbd but actual release 1-1.5 mbd; (9) Brent dropped from ~ to ~-105 on de-escalation hopes (Fortune/FinancialContent Apr 1). PATTERN: SPR release OPERATIONAL but IEA acknowledges INSUFFICIENT to offset sustained Gulf disruption. Structural supply deficit persists. Source: IndexBox/EnergyConnects/CNBC/EnergiasMedia/Fortune

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+28, SPR deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1, NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound RFP due Apr 6

fp1_china_action.label: 中国实质行动(唯一可行聚点注入)
fp1_china_action.status: ACTIVE MEDIATION — China-Pakistan 5-point plan presented, CNN/BBC confirm China broker positioning, Zhai Jun touring, PH/MY/TH added to friendly list, BUT Iran-Oman PERMANENT protocol BYPASSES China Hormuz clause
fp1_china_action.findings: Day 45. ★★★★★ CHINA BROKER STRENGTHENING BUT OMAN BYPASS: (1) CNN Apr 1: 'Is China positioning itself to become US-Iran peace broker?' — Zhai Jun actively touring region; (2) BBC Apr 1: 'China trying to play peacemaker — will it work?'; (3) China-Pakistan 5-point plan: ceasefire + peace talks + civilian protection + HORMUZ RESTORATION + overall framework (IndianExpress/Reuters); (4) China-Pakistan plan most CONCRETE diplomatic framework yet presented (Axios); (5) ★★★ BUT Iran Deputy FM Apr 2: drafting PERMANENT protocol with OMAN to oversee Hormuz even in peacetime (NYT) — BYPASSES China-Pakistan Hormuz clause; (6) Iran FM Araghchi: no ceasefire but 'complete end to war' — wider framing than China plan (AJE); (7) Iran seeking Russia+China as GUARANTORS not just mediators (ToI); (8) Witkoff: US+Iran engaging in both direct and mediated discussions (Wikipedia); (9) Friendly-nation list EXPANDING: Philippines added Apr 2 (after MY/TH) = China's economic corridor interest served even without formal mediation success. ASSESSMENT: China's INSTITUTIONAL positioning strengthening (media, plan, backchannel) but Iran acting INDEPENDENTLY on Hormuz sovereignty via Oman. China may end up as guarantor, not architect. Source: CNN/BBC/IndianExpress/Reuters/NYT/ToI/AJE/Axios

fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.label: 美方信号包内部矛盾(承诺解锁前导)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.status: MAXIMUM PARADOX — Trump 'nearing completion' + 'extremely hard 2-3 weeks' + bridge strike + Brent USD109+ Apr 3; NO Hormuz escort; Iran FM denies ALL negotiations; Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.findings: Day 45. ★★★★★ EXTREME CONTRADICTION PEAKS: (1) Trump primetime Apr 1: 'core objectives nearing completion', 'leaving very soon' (CNBC/NYT/AP); (2) Trump Apr 2: vows 'extremely hard over next 2-3 weeks' = CONTRADICTS wind-down (NYT); (3) US struck Iran LARGEST BRIDGE Karaj Apr 2 — 8 killed, 95 wounded = CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE while claiming near-victory (Guardian/BBC); (4) Brent SURGED past USD109 Apr 3, stocks tumble (BBC); spot Brent cargo USD141.36 = highest since 2008 (CNBC); (5) WTI spiked 11% to USD111.54 Apr 2 — market REJECTS de-escalation (CNBC); (6) Iran FM Araghchi: 'no negotiations, false and baseless' (AJE/USAToday/CNBC); (7) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — promised Mar 12, 5+ weeks unfulfilled; (8) ★★★ Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS: Trump paused energy plant destruction for 10 days until Apr 6 8PM ET — if no deal, strikes RESUME on energy infrastructure (Reuters Mar 26); (9) Macron criticizes shifting US goals (NYT); (10) Rep Landsman (D-OH) who voted AGAINST War Powers NOW calls for war end (WBUR Apr 2). ASSESSMENT: Maximum rhetoric-reality gap: victory claims + bridge strikes + record oil + zero escorts + Iran refusing talks + Apr 6 = BINARY inflection: deal or massive energy escalation. Source: BBC/Guardian/CNBC/NYT/AJE/USNI/Reuters/WBUR

fp3_face_saving_framework.label: 面子工程原材料出现
fp3_face_saving_framework.status: NO VIABLE EXIT — US 15-pt REJECTED, China 5-pt BYPASSED by Iran-Oman protocol, UNSC 2817 adopted but gridlocked, Iran: 'complete end not ceasefire' + Lebanon condition, UNSC Bahrain-backed draft vote imminent, Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS
fp3_face_saving_framework.findings: Day 45. ★★★★ UNSC ACTIVITY BUT ALL FRAMEWORKS FAILING: (1) UNSC Resolution 2817 adopted Mar 11 — demands Iran cease attacks + reaffirms Hormuz freedom of navigation (HouseOfCommons); (2) Guardian Apr 2: Bahrain FM urges UNSC 'unified position' for draft resolution vote (Guardian); (3) Iran REJECTED US 15-point plan — Trump claims Iran agreed to 'most of' it but Iran FM says 'false' (Wikipedia/AJE); (4) China-Pakistan 5-point plan BYPASSED — Iran's Oman protocol establishes PERMANENT Hormuz oversight without China (NYT Apr 2); (5) Russia ceasefire draft FAILED at UNSC (SecurityCouncilReport); (6) Iran insists Lebanon MUST be included in any deal (Wikipedia); (7) Iran FM: 'no ceasefire, complete end to war' — REJECTS ceasefire framing (AJE); (8) Iran seeking Russia+China as guarantors (ToI) — delays formal response; (9) UK Parliament Apr 2 briefing: US/Israel-Iran conflict, no clear diplomatic path; (10) Apr 6 energy deadline 3 DAYS = BINARY: deal or energy infrastructure strikes resume. ★★★ Emerging: multiple frameworks (US 15-pt, China 5-pt, UNSC, Oman protocol) = fragmented, NO single architecture. Source: HouseOfCommons/Guardian/Wikipedia/AJE/NYT/ToI/SecurityCouncilReport

fp4_cost_threshold.label: 成本阈值的物理逼近
fp4_cost_threshold.status: APPROACHING THRESHOLD — Gas USD4/gal national (+36% from pre-war), Brent USD109+ Apr 3, spot cargo USD141.36 (2008 high), US KIA 15 + 520+ wounded (Pentagon coverup), War Powers mid-Apr vote, bridge strike civilian backlash risk
fp4_cost_threshold.findings: Day 45. ★★★★★ COST SPIRAL + POLITICAL PRESSURE BUILDING: (1) Gas USD4/gal national (USAToday Apr 1) = highest since 2022, CA well over USD5 (NYT); (2) Brent SURGED past USD109 Apr 3 after Trump speech (BBC); (3) ★★★ Brent SPOT cargo hit USD141.36 = highest since 2008 financial crisis (CNBC Apr 2) — physical market premium EXTREME; (4) WTI spiked 11% to USD111.54 single day (CNBC Apr 2); (5) VIX ~24 (down from 32 peak but elevated); (6) S&P 500 6,575 (FRED Apr 1); (7) Gold USD4,578-4,720/oz (Fortune/ReelFinancial); (8) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded, Intercept Apr 1: Pentagon COVERING UP casualty figures; (9) Bridge strike Karaj: 8 killed, 95 wounded = CIVILIAN INFRASTRUCTURE — potential war crimes scrutiny + domestic backlash (Guardian/BBC); (10) War Powers vote DELAYED to mid-April — Congress on 2-week recess (DemocracyNow Mar 27); (11) Rep Landsman who voted AGAINST War Powers NOW calls for war end (WBUR) = erosion of bipartisan support; (12) Senate War Powers FAILED 53-47 party line (AJE). ASSESSMENT: USD4+ gas + record oil spot + casualty coverup + bridge strike footage = compounding pressure. Mid-April Congressional return + Apr 6 energy deadline = DUAL inflection risk. Source: USAToday/BBC/CNBC/NYT/Intercept/Guardian/WBUR/DemocracyNow/Fortune

noise_filter.rule: 按行为类型分类,不按人物分类
noise: 巴盖伊等发言人声明(言辞)
媒体'消息人士'报道(未验证)
signal: Trump签署行政令/军事命令(行动)
美军实际调兵/部署变化(行动)
IRGC实际攻击模式变化(行动)
中国实际经济/军事动作(行动)
noise_filter.key_distinction: 同一个人的言辞=噪声,行动=信号。Trump说'just for fun'=噪声。Trump命令打Kharg=信号。

pipeline_daily_checklist: 1. 物理层: 更新Q1(袭击频率) + Q2(通行量) + Q3(SPR释放)
2. 焦点层: 扫描fp1(中国动作) + fp2(美方矛盾) + fp3(退出框架) + fp4(成本阈值)
3. 噪声过滤: 新闻分类为action vs rhetoric
4. 结构性验证: 跑structural_verify检查盲区+停滞+链断裂
5. DAG更新: 只有物理层变化或焦点层信号触发才更新概率
